title: geum × macranthum
intro: Geum × macranthum, a hybrid species of flowering plant, is a cross between Geum coccineum and Geum rivale, commonly known as the large-flowered avens or burnet rose. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, and has been employed to treat various health conditions, such as wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Geum × macranthum include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate menstrual cramps. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve to harness its medicinal properties.
